name: 三亚
description: Sanya, the pearl of the South China Sea, is a tropical paradise on Hainan Island's southern coast. Famous for its year-round sunshine, crystal-clear waters, white sandy beaches, and lush tropical scenery, it is China's premier beach resort destination. The city offers a unique blend of natural beauty, ethnic culture, and modern luxury resorts.
highlights: Yalong Bay - The tropical paradise beach
Wuzhizhou Island - Scuba diving and water sports
Nanshan Cultural Tourism Zone - Giant Guanyin statue
Tianya Haijiao - End of the earth romance
Atlantis Sanya - Luxury resort and water park

climate.tips: Sanya enjoys warm weather year-round. Best visited from November to March when temperatures are pleasant and rainfall is low. Summer (June-August) is hot and humid with occasional typhoons. Pack sunscreen, sunglasses, and light clothing regardless of season.

quickFacts.visa: Visa-free for many countries, 30-day stay for tourism

attractions.name: 天涯海角
attractions.description: The iconic romantic destination literally meaning 'the ends of the earth and sea.' Famous for its massive stone monuments inscribed with calligraphic characters, pristine beaches, and romantic sunsets over the South China Sea.
attractions.tips: Visit in late afternoon for best sunset photos. Walk to the far end of the beach for less crowds. Buy water before entering as prices are higher inside.

attractions.name: 亚龙湾
attractions.description: Known as the 'first bay of China' with 7km of white sandy beach, crystal-clear waters, and coral reefs. Home to luxury resorts and excellent water sports facilities.
attractions.tips: Best beach for swimming in Sanya. Bring your own snorkeling gear to save money. Arrive early to secure good sun loungers. Most hotels have private beach access.

attractions.name: 大东海
attractions.description: A popular public beach in Sanya city center, offering convenient access, calm waters, and vibrant nightlife nearby. Suitable for swimming and sunbathing.
attractions.tips: Good for evening strolls and sunset views. Be aware of strong currents - swim only in designated areas. Evening beach bars come alive after sunset.

attractions.name: 南山文化旅游区
attractions.description: A massive Buddhist cultural complex featuring the iconic 108-meter tall Guanyin statue standing in the sea. Combines Buddhist art, traditional architecture, and ocean views.
attractions.tips: Allow full morning or afternoon for this large complex. Wear comfortable walking shoes. Respect Buddhist customs - dress modestly. Don't miss the vegetarian restaurant.

attractions.name: 蜈支洲岛
attractions.description: A tropical island paradise renowned for its crystal-clear waters, colorful coral reefs, and diverse marine life. Offers world-class scuba diving, snorkeling, and water sports.
attractions.tips: Book ferry tickets online in advance during peak season. Bring waterproof bag for phone. Underwater activities best in morning. Stay on island until last ferry for sunset.

attractions.name: 鹿回头公园
attractions.description: A hilltop park overlooking Sanya Bay with a famous legend of a deer and hunter turned into stone. Offers panoramic views of the city, bay, and surrounding mountains.
attractions.tips: Best visited at sunset for romantic views. Take the观光车 up the hill to save energy. Night view of Sanya city lights is spectacular. Combine with Dadonghai nearby.

attractions.name: 大小洞天
attractions.description: A Taoist scenic area featuring ancient stone formations, coastal caves, and clear waters. Known as the 'first bestscenic spot in Hainan' with over 800 years of Taoist history.
attractions.tips: Less crowded than Tianya Haijiao. Best for photography and nature lovers. Combine with Nanshan for a full day cultural tour.

attractions.name: 呀诺达雨林
attractions.description: A pristine tropical rainforest park offering hiking trails, waterfalls, and authentic Li and Miao ethnic experiences. Known for its oxygen-rich air and natural waterfalls.
attractions.tips: Bring rain gear regardless of weather - rainforest is humid. Wear non-slip shoes for waterfall trails. Book a guide for better cultural insights. Best visited in morning.

attractions.name: 槟榔谷
attractions.nameEn: Binglanggu Li and Miao Cultural Park
attractions.description: A cultural heritage park showcasing the traditional lifestyles of Hainan's Li and Miao ethnic minorities. Features original villages, handicraft workshops, and cultural performances.
attractions.tips: Book through travel agencies for better prices. Watch the afternoon cultural show. Learn basic phrases in Li language. Respect local customs and photography restrictions.

attractions.name: 三亚湾
attractions.description: The longest beach in Sanya, stretching 22km along the city coastline. Famous for its coconut groves, sunset views, and the iconic 'Crystal Princess' sculpture.
attractions.tips: Best at sunset for golden hour photos. Less touristy than Yalong Bay. Great for early morning tai chi. Popular with locals on weekends.

attractions.name: 海棠湾
attractions.description: A pristine bay home to luxury resorts and the famous Atlantis Sanya resort. Features quieter beaches, shopping at Sanya International免税城, and world-class amenities.
attractions.tips: Best for resort-style relaxation. Atlantis Aquaventure water park is a must-visit. Use resort shuttles or taxi to get around. Hotel beaches are cleaner but limited.

attractions.name: 亚特兰蒂斯
attractions.description: A legendary luxury integrated resort featuring the Aquaventure water park,失落的空间水族馆, and the world's largest免税城. Iconic architecture and entertainment complex.
attractions.ticketPrice: 298¥ (water park day pass), 398¥ (水族馆+水公园)
attractions.tips: Book tickets online for discounts. Bring waterproof phone case. Rent cabanas for rest breaks. Visit on weekdays to avoid crowds.

attractions.name: 南山寺
attractions.description: A magnificent Buddhist temple within Nanshan Cultural Zone, featuring traditional Chinese Buddhist architecture, beautiful gardens, and the towering Guanyin statue.
attractions.tips: Part of Nanshan Cultural Zone - buy combined ticket. Respect temple etiquette. Morning visits are more peaceful. Don't miss the vegetarian restaurant.

attractions.name: 海上观音
attractions.description: The iconic 108-meter tall Guanyin (Avalokitesvara) statue standing majestically in the sea. One of China's tallest religious statues and a symbol of Sanya.
attractions.tips: Best photo opportunities from the bridge. Walk around for different views. Lightning rod on top is striking during storms. Combine with entire Nanshan zone.

attractions.name: 大东海
attractions.description: The most popular beach in Sanya city area with gentle waves suitable for swimming. Backed by hotels, restaurants, and bars with easy access from downtown.
attractions.tips: Swim between lifeguard flags. Rent jet skis on beach. Evening beach bars and fire dancing shows. Negotiate prices for water activities.

attractions.name: 小洞天
attractions.description: Part of the larger Daxiao Dongtian scenic area featuring smaller coastal caves and rock formations with Taoist significance. Less crowded than main area.
attractions.tips: Often combined with Daxiao Dongtian visit. Good for photography. Peaceful atmosphere. Bring hiking shoes for rocky paths.

attractions.name: 万寿 Dongtian
attractions.description: An ancient Taoist cultivation site with stone inscriptions dating back to the Song Dynasty. Features unique rock formations and peaceful mountain trails.
attractions.tips: Combine with nearby attractions. Wear comfortable shoes for hiking. Less known by tourists - peaceful experience. Bring water and snacks.

attractions.name: 凤凰岭公园
attractions.description: The highest observation point in Sanya offering 360-degree panoramic views of the entire bay area. Features crystal viewing platform and sunset views.
attractions.tips: Best visited at sunset for dramatic views. Take cable car both ways to save energy. Bring jacket as it can be windy at top. Night view of Sanya is spectacular.

attractions.name: 中免三亚国际免税城
attractions.description: The world's largest duty-free shopping complex with luxury brands, cosmetics, electronics, and designer goods. Up to 30% savings on international brands.
attractions.tips: Bring passport for duty-free shopping. Download CDF app for exclusive deals. Plan visit during sales events. Don't forget to pick up your purchases at airport.

attractions.name: 珠江南田温泉
attractions.description: A premier hot spring resort with over 50 mineral-rich pools set in tropical gardens. Features various themed pools, spa treatments, and relaxation facilities.
attractions.tips: Book evening slot for romantic atmosphere. Try fish foot massage first. Bring swimsuit and flip-flops. Many pools are outdoors - sunscreen essential.

attractions.name: 天涯海角婚庆节
attractions.description: The famous romantic destination where couples declare their love at the edge of the world. Known for wedding ceremonies, love locks, and romantic traditions.
attractions.tips: Popular with couples - romantic experience. Watch traditional wedding ceremonies at 10:00 and 15:00. Lock love padlocks on fence. Sunset proposal spot.

attractions.name: 落笔洞
attractions.description: An ancient limestone cave with stalactites and inscriptions dating back thousands of years. One of the oldest caves in Hainan.
attractions.tips: Less crowded than other attractions. Combine with nearby Yanuo Rainforest.

attractions.name: 红树林自然保护区
attractions.description: Protected mangrove ecosystem offering eco-tours and bird watching. Home to diverse marine and bird species.
attractions.tips: Best visited in morning for wildlife. Bring binoculars for bird watching.

attractions.name: 鸿港渔港
attractions.description: Working fishing port where you can watch daily fish auctions and buy the freshest catch directly from fishermen.
attractions.tips: Visit early morning for the busiest auction. Bring camera for colorful scenes. Bargain for prices.

attractions.name: 热带天堂森林公园
attractions.description: Famous as the filming location for TV drama 'Love in the City'. Offers rainforest hiking and panoramic views from the non-free-fall zone.
attractions.tips: Famous from 'Love in the City' drama. Book cable car in advance. Wear comfortable shoes.

attractions.name: 三亚海昌梦幻不夜城
attractions.description: A large entertainment complex with themed rides, shows, and dining. Features the famous Ferris wheel overlooking the bay.
attractions.tips: Best in the evening. Ferris wheel has best sunset views. Shows start at 19:00.

attractions.name: 三亚中央海景大道
attractions.description: A scenic coastal promenade connecting major beaches with palm-lined walkways, cafes, and sunset views.
attractions.tips: Best for evening strolls. Find local food stalls along the way. Great for photos.

attractions.name: 临春岭森林公园
attractions.description: A hilltop park with hiking trails and observation deck offering views of Sanya Bay and city skyline.
attractions.tips: Best visited at sunrise for panoramic views. Good for exercise. Cooler in the mornings.

attractions.name: 亚龙湾
attractions.description: 中国最美的海湾之一，有细软沙滩和清澈海水。
attractions.tips: 夏季最佳。

attractions.name: 三亚湾
attractions.description: 三亚最长的海滩，有椰梦长廊。
attractions.tips: 傍晚看日落。

attractions.name: 天涯海角
attractions.description: 中国最著名的海滨景点，有天涯和海角石刻。
attractions.tips: 三亚必打卡。

attractions.name: 南山文化旅游区
attractions.description: 有108米高的海上观音像。
attractions.tips: 了解佛教文化。

attractions.name: 蜈支洲岛
attractions.description: 三亚最美的海岛，有丰富的水上项目。
attractions.tips: 夏季最佳。

attractions.name: 海棠湾
attractions.description: 三亚新开发的海湾，有免税购物中心。
attractions.tips: 购物和海滩。

attractions.name: 鹿回头
attractions.description: 三亚的城市公园，可俯瞰三亚全景。
attractions.tips: 看三亚全景。

attractions.name: 三亚美食
attractions.description: 三亚有新鲜海鲜和热带水果。
attractions.tips: 品尝三亚海鲜。

attractions.name: 三亚夜景
attractions.description: 三亚湾的夜景灯光秀。
attractions.tips: 夏季夜间最美。

attractions.name: 三亚博物馆
attractions.description: 展示三亚历史的博物馆。
attractions.tips: 了解三亚文化。

attractions.name: 大东海
attractions.description: 三亚最著名的海滩之一。
attractions.tips: 夏季最佳。

attractions.name: 热带天堂森林公园
attractions.description: 三亚的热带雨林公园。
attractions.tips: 了解热带生态。

attractions.name: 西岛
attractions.description: 三亚附近的海岛，有丰富的水上项目。
attractions.tips: 夏季最佳。

attractions.name: 呀诺达雨林
attractions.description: 三亚的热带雨林景区。
attractions.tips: 了解热带生态。

attractions.name: 第一市场
attractions.description: 三亚最著名的海鲜市场。
attractions.tips: 买海鲜加工。

attractions.name: 南山寺
attractions.description: 南山文化旅游区内的佛教寺院。
attractions.tips: 了解佛教文化。

attractions.name: 崖州湾
attractions.description: 三亚西部的海湾，人少安静。
attractions.tips: 人少安静。

attractions.name: 三亚千古情
attractions.description: 以三亚文化为主题的大型公园。
attractions.tips: 观看千古情演出。

attractions.name: 亚龙湾潜水
attractions.description: 三亚最好的潜水点之一。
attractions.tips: 体验潜水。

attractions.name: 三亚热带水果
attractions.description: 三亚有各种热带水果。
attractions.tips: 品尝热带水果。

attractions.name: 三亚温泉
attractions.description: 三亚有丰富的温泉资源。
attractions.tips: 泡温泉。

attractions.name: 三亚宋城
attractions.description: 以三亚文化为主题的大型公园。
attractions.tips: 观看千古情演出。

restaurants.name: 第一市场海鲜
restaurants.description: The most famous seafood market in Sanya. Buy fresh seafood from vendors and have it cooked at neighboring restaurants for a small fee.

restaurants.name: 大悦洋海鲜
restaurants.description: Popular seafood hotpot restaurant with fresh catch. Known for their ocean hotpot and all-you-can-eat seafood options.

restaurants.name: 椰子鸡
restaurants.description: Signature Hainan dish featuring tender chicken cooked in fresh coconut juice. Sweet, nutritious, and perfect for tropical weather.

restaurants.name: 抱罗粉
restaurants.description: Famous Hainan rice noodle dish with a rich peanut and soy sauce broth. Topped with pickled vegetables and ground pork.

restaurants.name: 清补凉
restaurants.description: Refreshing Hainan dessert soup with glutinous rice balls, red beans, lotus seeds, and coconut milk. Perfect for hot weather.

restaurants.name: 海南鸡饭
restaurants.description: Classic Hainan dish of poached chicken served with fragrant rice cooked in chicken broth. Served with chili sauce and ginger paste.

restaurants.name: 海鲜自助
restaurants.description: All-you-can-eat seafood buffet with fresh lobster, crab, shrimp, and various grilled specialties. Excellent value for seafood lovers.

restaurants.name: 热带水果
restaurants.description: Fresh tropical fruits including mangoes, pineapples, dragon fruit, lychee, and durian at local prices. Great for snacks and gifts.

restaurants.name: 糟粕醋火锅
restaurants.description: Unique Hainan fermented rice hotpot with a sour and spicy taste. Loaded with seafood, vegetables, and tofu.

restaurants.name: 陵水酸粉
restaurants.description: Famous sour noodles from Lingshui County featuring fine rice noodles in sour broth with special toppings.

restaurants.name: 闻绮法餐厅
restaurants.description: Michelin-starred French fine dining at Atlantis with ocean views and exceptional service. Perfect for special occasions.

restaurants.name: 鮨樱
restaurants.description: Premium Japanese restaurant with fresh Hainan seafood prepared in traditional Japanese style. Omakase menu available.

restaurants.name: 火车头万人海鲜广场
restaurants.description: Large seafood plaza with many vendors and cooking stations. Fresh selection with transparent pricing.

restaurants.name: 利思酒家
restaurants.description: Established Hainan restaurant serving traditional local dishes with quality ingredients and authentic recipes.

restaurants.name: 海角轩
restaurants.description: Beachfront fine dining at the Ritz-Carlton offering international cuisine with stunning ocean views.

restaurants.name: 海边烧烤
restaurants.description: Beachside BBQ stalls offering grilled seafood and meats. Casual atmosphere with sand between your toes.

restaurants.name: 南山素斋
restaurants.description: Vegetarian restaurant within Nanshan offering Buddhist-style meat alternatives made from tofu and vegetables.

restaurants.name: 印象半山
restaurants.description: Upscale Hainan fusion restaurant on the mountain with panoramic bay views. Known for creative local dishes.

restaurants.name: 东山羊
restaurants.description: Famous for Dongshan lamb - a prized Hainan breed known for its tender meat and mild flavor. Various cooking styles available.

restaurants.name: 疍家渔排
restaurants.description: Unique dining experience on floating fishing rafts. Fresh seafood directly from the net to your table.

restaurants.name: 吉姆餐厅
restaurants.description: Popular family restaurant serving local Hainan dishes with generous portions.

restaurants.name: 钟楼老字号
restaurants.description: Established local restaurant near the famous bell tower. Known for traditional recipes.

restaurants.name: 粤菜馆
restaurants.description: Fine Cantonese cuisine near Yalong Bay resorts. Premium dim sum and fresh seafood.

restaurants.name: 后安粉汤
restaurants.description: Famous local breakfast spot serving Houan rice noodles in rich pork bone broth.

restaurants.name: 生蚝烧烤
restaurants.description: Famous oyster and seafood BBQ stall at Sanya Bay night market.

restaurants.name: 和乐蟹
restaurants.description: Specializing in Wanning Hele Crab - one of Hainan's most prized seafood.

restaurants.name: 文昌鸡饭店
restaurants.description: Budget-friendly Wenchang chicken rice from famous Hainan chicken chain.

restaurants.name: 首尔烤肉
restaurants.description: Popular Korean BBQ restaurant with all-you-can-eat options.

restaurants.name: 鸿港市场海鲜
restaurants.description: Local market seafood - buy fish and have it cooked at adjacent restaurants.

restaurants.name: 鹿回头黎家菜
restaurants.description: Authentic Li ethnic cuisine with wild vegetables and traditional cooking methods.

restaurants.name: 阳光西餐厅
restaurants.description: Beachfront Western restaurant at luxury resort serving international cuisine.

hotels.name: 亚特兰蒂斯酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Book well in advance for holiday periods. Package deals include water park tickets. Club rooms offer better perks.

hotels.name: 瑞吉度假酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Best for couples and special occasions. Request oceanview rooms. Resort credit included in some packages.

hotels.name: 海棠湾君悦酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Great for families. Kids club activities daily. Book family suites for more space. Check for package deals.

hotels.name: 文华东方酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Best for honeymooners. Stunning sea views from cliff rooms. Award-winning spa. Intimate atmosphere.

hotels.name: 铂尔曼度假酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Best value in Sanya Bay. Great for business travelers. Free shuttle to attractions. Book seaview rooms.

hotels.name: 希尔顿酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Good balance of price and quality. Multiple dining options. Kids club available. Book family room for space.

hotels.name: 康莱德酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Modern and stylish. Great pool area. Book lagoon access rooms. Good for younger travelers.

hotels.name: 万豪酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Best location for exploring city. Walking distance to Dadonghai. Good breakfast included. Book oceanview.

hotels.name: 三亚湾皇冠假日酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Good value option. Beach access. Close to city center. Free parking available.

hotels.name: 亚龙湾迎宾馆
hotels.bookingTips: Best budget option in Yalong Bay. Walk to beach. Simple but clean. Book early for best rates.

hotels.name: 大东海海景酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Great for budget travelers. Steps to beach. Walking distance to restaurants. Request high floor for views.

hotels.name: 蜈支洲岛珊瑚酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Stay on the island for exclusivity. Book ferry tickets together. No star ratings but full experience. Peaceful retreat.

hotels.name: 三亚中心皇冠酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Best for quick stays. Very central. Basic but clean. Walking distance to markets.

hotels.name: 香水湾君澜度假酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Perfect for honeymooners. Extremely private. Beautiful scenery. Rent a car recommended.

hotels.name: 海棠湾9号度假酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Best value in Haitang Bay. Near duty-free shopping. Shuttle to beach. Good for shoppers.

hotels.name: 三亚湾海韵度假酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Best for families. Book ocean-view rooms. Good value for the price.

hotels.name: 三亚艾迪逊酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Best for young travelers. Instagram-worthy. Book beachfront villa.

hotels.name: 大东海银泰阳光度假村
hotels.bookingTips: Good location for exploring. Request high floor for better views.

hotels.name: 亚龙湾仙人掌度假酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Good family option. Budget-friendly for Yalong Bay. Kids club available.

hotels.name: 三亚国际饭店
hotels.bookingTips: Best for business travelers. Convenient location. Consistent quality.

hotels.name: 香水湾富力万豪酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Best for golf lovers. Quiet location. Rent a car recommended.

emergencyContacts.name: 三亚市公安局
emergencyContacts.notes: 24-hour service. English interpretation may be limited.

emergencyContacts.name: 解放军总医院海南分院
中国外交部领事保护
emergencyContacts.notes: 24-hour consular protection for Chinese citizens abroad or foreigners in China.

emergencyContacts.name: 三亚旅游信息咨询
emergencyContacts.notes: Tourist information, maps, and assistance. English available.

加拿大驻广州总领事馆
新加坡驻广州总领事馆
三亚旅游信息咨询中心
emergencyContacts.notes: Tourist information, maps, and assistance. English available.

culturalTips.title: Beach Etiquette
culturalTips.content: Sanya's beaches can get crowded. Don't set up umbrellas too close to others. Beach chairs at resorts are usually reserved for hotel guests. Public beaches are shared spaces - keep them clean.

culturalTips.title: Sun Protection is Essential
culturalTips.content: The tropical sun is intense year-round. Apply SPF 50+ sunscreen regularly, wear hats and sunglasses, and seek shade during peak hours (11:00-15:00). Sunburns can ruin your vacation.

culturalTips.title: Bargaining at Markets
culturalTips.content: At local markets and street vendors, bargaining is expected and encouraged. Start at 30-40% of the asking price and negotiate up. Be friendly - it's a social activity.

culturalTips.title: Seafood Market Protocol
culturalTips.content: At seafood markets like First Market, compare prices between vendors before buying. Bring your purchases to adjacent restaurants for a small cooking fee (usually 5-15¥ per dish).

culturalTips.title: Island Day Trip Planning
culturalTips.content: For popular islands like Wuzhizhou, arrive early (before 09:00) to avoid long ferry queues. Book ferry tickets online. Bring cash, waterproof bags, and sunscreen.

culturalTips.title: Monsoon Season Awareness
culturalTips.content: May to October is monsoon season with more rain and potential typhoons. Monitor weather forecasts. Outdoor activities may be affected. Have backup plans for indoor attractions.

culturalTips.title: Resort Beach Access
culturalTips.content: Luxury resort beaches are usually private and exclusive to guests. If visiting for the day, use public beaches like Dadonghai or Sanya Bay to avoid being turned away.

culturalTips.title: Duty-Free Shopping Tips
culturalTips.content: The CDF duty-free mall in Haitang Bay offers up to 30% off international brands. Shop for at least 800¥ to qualify. Pick up purchases at the airport on departure.

culturalTips.title: Respect Buddhist Customs
culturalTips.content: When visiting Nanshan Temple and other religious sites, dress modestly (cover shoulders and knees). Don't pose inappropriately with Buddha statues. Light incense with both hands.

culturalTips.title: Water Safety
culturalTips.content: Only swim at designated beach areas with lifeguards. Strong currents can be dangerous outside marked zones. Don't swim after drinking alcohol. Heed red flag warnings.

culturalTips.title: Local Transport Options
culturalTips.content: Taxis are the most convenient transport in Sanya. Use DiDi (Chinese Uber) for reliable pricing. Bus 24 and 25 are tourist-friendly routes. Rent a car for flexibility.

culturalTips.title: Mosquito and Insect Precautions
culturalTips.content: Tropical insects are prevalent, especially in rainforest areas like Yanuo. Bring insect repellent. Use蚊香 (mosquito coil) in hotel rooms. Anti-itch cream is essential.

culturalTips.title: Beach Photography Ethics
culturalTips.content: Don't photograph locals without permission, especially fishermen and market vendors. At popular photo spots, take turns politely. Avoid blocking paths for photos.

culturalTips.title: Hotel Check-in Culture
culturalTips.content: Check-in is usually 14:00 and checkout is 12:00. International hotels are strict about times. Request late checkout in advance. Bring passport for registration.

culturalTips.title: Hainan Tea Culture
culturalTips.content: Hainan people love their早茶 (morning tea). Join locals at tea houses from 07:00-10:00 for dim sum, congee, and tea. It's a social ritual and great for culture immersion.

culturalTips.title: Peak Season Planning
culturalTips.content: Chinese holidays (National Day, Chinese New Year) bring massive crowds. Book hotels and attractions months in advance. Consider shoulder season (March-May, September-November) for fewer tourists.

culturalTips.title: Hainan Coffee Culture
culturalTips.content: Hainan has a unique coffee culture dating back to the 1930s. Try the local 'Gah Fei' (Nanyang style coffee) at old cafes in the city. It's sweet, strong, and served with condensed milk.

culturalTips.title: Li and Miao Heritage
culturalTips.content: Hainan is home to Li (80%) and Miao ethnic minorities. Visit Binglanggu Cultural Park to learn about their traditions. The Li are known for their colorful textiles and traditional tattoo art.

culturalTips.title: Seafood Pricing Tips
culturalTips.content: When buying seafood at markets, prices vary widely. Compare prices between vendors. Ask about weight before purchasing. The cooking fee is usually 5-15¥ per dish. Watch the scale when weighing.

culturalTips.title: Beach Safety Flags
culturalTips.content: Sanya beaches use colored flags for safety: Green = safe to swim, Yellow = swim with caution, Red = dangerous, no swimming. Heed lifeguard warnings. Currents can be strong even near shore.

culturalTips.title: Coconut Culture
culturalTips.content: Sanya is known for coconuts. Fresh coconut juice (椰子水) is sold everywhere for 8-15¥. Try coconut ice cream, coconut milk, and coconut-based dishes. The coconut groves of Sanya Bay are iconic.

culturalTips.title: Typhoon Season
culturalTips.content: Typhoons are possible from May to November, peaking in August-September. Check weather forecasts daily. Outdoor attractions may close during storms. Have indoor backup plans.

culturalTips.title: Island Hopping
culturalTips.content: Besides Wuzhizhou Island, there are other islands like Phoenix Island and Dongshan Island. Book through licensed tour operators. Ferry schedules depend on weather conditions.

culturalTips.title: Nightlife Scene
culturalTips.content: Dadonghai has the best nightlife scene with beach bars, live music, and fire dancing shows. Haitang Bay is quieter but growing. Yalong Bay has less nightlife but more upscale options.

culturalTips.title: Tipping Culture
culturalTips.content: Tipping is not expected or common in Sanya. Service charges are rarely added to bills. At upscale resorts, some international staff may accept tips. It's never required.

culturalTips.title: Photography at Scenic Spots
culturalTips.content: Popular photo spots at Tianya Haijiao and Luhuitou can get very crowded. Professional photographers offer services for a fee. Go early morning or late afternoon for fewer crowds.

culturalTips.title: Durian Season
culturalTips.content: Durian season in Hainan is June-August. Prices are lower than imported varieties. The smell is intense - not all hotel rooms allow durian inside. Buy from reputable vendors to ensure quality.

culturalTips.title: Snorkeling and Diving Ethics
culturalTips.content: Don't touch coral or marine life when snorkeling or diving. Some operators may encourage standing on coral - this is destructive. Choose eco-certified dive operators. Take only photos.

payment.description: China's dominant mobile payment platform, accepted everywhere in Sanya from luxury hotels to street vendors.
howToUse: Download Alipay app and link your bank card or credit card
Scan QR codes with the app to pay
Some merchants support international cards (Alipay HK, Ko莉卡)
tips: Most effective with Chinese bank account or Alipay HK for visitors
Keep phone charged - essential for all payments
Show merchant the payment screen as proof

payment.description: The other major mobile payment in China, integrated with the WeChat messaging app. Essential for WeChat-based services.
howToUse: Open WeChat, link bank card through 我-Pay
Scan merchant QR code or show your code to be scanned
Works for WeChat mini-programs and services
tips: International version WeChat Pay supports foreign cards
Essential for ordering delivery and ride-hailing
Keep sufficient balance or linked funds

payment.description: Chinese Yuan cash is still useful at local markets, small vendors, and tips. ATMs are widely available.
howToUse: Withdraw from ATMs using international cards (China Bank, ICBC, etc.)
ATMs accept Visa, Mastercard, and UnionPay
Exchange currency at airports or banks
tips: Bring small denominations for markets and tips
ATMs at 7-Eleven and banks are most reliable
Some smaller restaurants only accept cash

payment.description: China's domestic card network. Cards with UnionPay logo work at ATMs and merchants accepting bank cards.
howToUse: Use bank cards with UnionPay logo at ATMs
Insert card and enter PIN at POS terminals
Widely accepted at hotels, malls, and restaurants
tips: Most reliable card option in China
Check with your bank about international usage

payment.description: Visa and Mastercard are accepted at luxury hotels, major malls, and international chain restaurants. Less common at local establishments.
howToUse: Look for Visa/Mastercard logos at merchants
Insert card and sign for purchases
Some places may add foreign card surcharges
tips: Notify your bank before traveling to prevent fraud blocks
Best used at large merchants and duty-free shops
Keep card details secure - some machines are manual

payment.description: Rarely accepted in modern Sanya. Only some hotels and large tourist establishments might accept them.
howToUse: Exchange at banks for cash or UnionPay
tips: Mobile payments are far more convenient

arrival.tips: Direct flights from most major Chinese cities. Sanya airport is being expanded. Book early during peak season.
Several flights daily. Consider Shanghai Hongqiao for shorter transfer. High-speed train from Shanghai is also an option.
Shortest flight time. Most convenient for Guangdong visitors. Book day-of sometimes cheaper.
Beautiful coastal route if taking the slow train. High-speed train via Haikou is faster. Book through 12306 app.
Take high-speed train to Haikou, then ferry or train to Sanya. Scenic but long journey. Consider flying for time efficiency.
Expressway route. Air-conditioned coaches available. Book at bus stations or through app. Slower than train but cheaper.
Combined with train journey from Guangzhou. Scenic South China Sea crossing. Book through 粤海铁 app.

metro: No operational metro as of 2024 - rely on buses and taxis
bus: Bus 24: Dadonghai - Yalong Bay - Nanshan (Tourist Line)
Bus 25: Sanya Bay - Nanshan - Tianya Haijiao
Bus 15: Sanya Railway Station - Sanya Bay
Bus 35: City Center - Yalong Bay Resort
Tips: Exact change needed, or use WeChat mini-program for digital tickets
taxi: Standard taxis start at 8¥, 2.5¥ per km
DiDi (Chinese Uber) via WeChat or DiDi app - most reliable
Luxury limo services available through hotels
Airport taxis: Official taxi stand at terminal exit
Tips: Negotiate price for longer trips or confirm meter is used
bike: Shared bike services (Hellobike, Meituan) available near tourist areas
Deposit required via WeChat or Alipay
Be careful cycling in summer heat - dangerous during midday
Not recommended for long distances in Sanya's climate
